one_time_description: Robinson Huron Treaty settlement receivable from Federal and Ontario Provincial Governments for past compensation obligations under the Robinson Huron Treaty of 1850, arising from claims filed in 2012
economic_notes: GBEs include Z'gamok Enterprises Inc., OCL Custom Crushing and Contracting Ltd., Z'gamok Construction Limited Partnership, and Robinson Huron Forestry Company Inc. Partnerships include Great Lakes Hospitality Group (11.78% interest, hotel operations) and Aki-eh Dibiniwewziwin Limited Partnership (mining, relinquished during year). During the year $14,458,932 in GBE loans were converted to equity in ZCLP ($4,333,230) and OCL ($10,125,702).
notes: Dominant item is $603,516,220 Robinson Huron Treaty settlement receivable recorded in 2024, making revenue and surplus appear extremely large. This is a one-time non-recurring settlement. Total accumulated surplus jumped from $53.9M to $683.6M due to this item. Two fully consolidated subsidiaries: Sagamok Development Corporation and 2344827 Ontario Inc. (Ritchie Falls Resort). Four GBEs on modified equity basis: ZEI, ZCLP, OCL, RHF. Long-term debt includes $18,878,404 interim financing from First Nation Financing Authority. Prior year audit was conducted by a different auditor (unqualified opinion November 29, 2023). OSR calculation includes the treaty settlement amount and GBE income as non-government-transfer items; total government transfers summed from named government sources in revenue lines.
